chapter_goal: Explain the Abel-Jacobi invariant as a secondary measurement for homologically trivial cycles.
checks: lattice reduction is invariant under changing the bounding chain by a cycle
primary class flag is zero before Abel-Jacobi is evaluated
lab: Change a bounding chain by an integral cycle and verify that the Abel-Jacobi point is unchanged on the torus.
library_routes: Matplotlib for torus quotient and path diagrams
pitfalls: using Abel-Jacobi on a cycle with nonzero primary class
treating Deligne cohomology as ordinary de Rham cohomology
proof_moves: quotient a complex vector space by a Hodge subspace and a lattice
integrate forms over a chain bounding the cycle
check indeterminacy is exactly the period lattice
visual_focus: A torus with a chain-integral vector shows how Abel-Jacobi records secondary period data.
